req.title: Manager deletes benefit
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to delete a benefit through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers UI-driven benefit deletion by an authenticated manager. I derived: (1) a positive EP/STC test confirming happy-path deletion, audit record creation, and 200/201 response; (2) a NEG/DT test for unauthenticated access expecting 401; (3) a NEG/DT test for a forbidden role (employee) expecting 403; (4) a BVA boundary test targeting the minimum valid benefit_id (1), verifying the delete still succeeds; (5) a NEG/NEG test supplying an invalid/missing benefit_id (0 or empty) to trigger a 400 validation error. The violation in test[4] was that kind=negative but heuristic=BVA, which is contradictory - BVA applies to boundary tests. The fix changes test[4] heuristic from BVA to NEG to match the negative kind, since supplying an out-of-range/invalid id is a negative equivalence partition test, not a boundary value test.

tests.name: test_manager_deletes_benefit_happy_path
preconditions: A benefit record exists in the system
actions: Obtain manager auth token via <get_auth_token> fixture
Navigate to /benefits and submit delete form for the created benefit
expected: Response body contains the deleted entity id
Benefit no longer appears in /benefits list
Audit record for the deletion exists via <get_audit_record>
trace: R-WEB-035: audit record written on success
R-WEB-035: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_manager_deletes_benefit_happy_path(
    manager_page,        # <fixture>: authenticated Playwright Page as manager
    create_benefit,      # <fixture>: creates benefit, yields benefit_id, deletes on teardown if still exists
    api_client,          # <fixture>: authenticated requests.Session
    benefits_url,        # <fixture>: base URL + '/benefits'
):
    benefit_id = create_benefit(name="Health Plan Beta")
    page = manager_page
    page.goto(benefits_url)
    row = page.locator(f"[data-benefit-id='{benefit_id}']")
    row.locator("button[data-action='delete']").click()
    page.locator("button[data-testid='confirm-delete']").click()
    expect(page.locator("[data-testid='toast-success']")).to_be_visible()
    resp = api_client.get(f"{benefits_url}/{benefit_id}")
    assert resp.status_code == 404, "Benefit should no longer exist"
    audit = <get_audit_record>(api_client, entity="benefit", entity_id=benefit_id, action="delete")
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record must be written"
    assert audit["entity_id"] == str(benefit_id)

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_delete_benefit
preconditions: No auth token is present (unauthenticated session)
A benefit record exists in the system
actions: Attempt DELETE request to /benefits/{id} without Authorization header
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_delete_benefit(
    create_benefit,      # <fixture>: creates benefit, yields benefit_id
    benefits_url,        # <fixture>: base URL + '/benefits'
    api_client,          # <fixture>: authenticated requests.Session (for verification only)
):
    benefit_id = create_benefit(name="Dental Plan Anon")
    anon_session = requests.Session()
    resp = anon_session.delete(f"{benefits_url}/{benefit_id}")
    assert resp.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401, got {resp.status_code}"
    verify = api_client.get(f"{benefits_url}/{benefit_id}")
    assert verify.status_code == 200, "Benefit must still exist after rejected delete"
    audit = <get_audit_record>(api_client, entity="benefit", entity_id=benefit_id, action="delete")
    assert audit is None, "No audit record should exist for rejected delete"

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_cannot_delete_benefit
preconditions: User is authenticated but has role 'employee' (not manager)
A benefit record exists in the system
actions: Obtain employee auth token via <get_auth_token> with role=employee
Attempt DELETE request to /benefits/{id} with employee token
trace: R-WEB-035: error pathway - 403 if role forbidden
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_forbidden_role_cannot_delete_benefit(
    employee_api_client, # <fixture>: requests.Session authenticated as role=employee
    create_benefit,      # <fixture>: creates benefit, yields benefit_id
    api_client,          # <fixture>: manager requests.Session for verification
    benefits_url,        # <fixture>: base URL + '/benefits'
):
    benefit_id = create_benefit(name="Vision Plan Forbidden")
    resp = employee_api_client.delete(f"{benefits_url}/{benefit_id}")
    assert resp.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "forbidden" in body.get("message", "").lower() or "permission" in body.get("message", "").lower()
    verify = api_client.get(f"{benefits_url}/{benefit_id}")
    assert verify.status_code == 200, "Benefit must still exist after 403"
    audit = <get_audit_record>(api_client, entity="benefit", entity_id=benefit_id, action="delete")
    assert audit is None, "No audit record should exist for a forbidden delete attempt"

tests.name: test_manager_deletes_benefit_with_minimum_valid_id
preconditions: A benefit exists whose ID is the minimum allowed positive integer (1)
actions: Seed or locate the benefit with id=1 via <ensure_benefit_with_id_fixture>
Submit DELETE request for benefit_id=1 using manager credentials
expected: Response body contains entity id equal to 1
trace: R-WEB-035: boundary - minimum valid benefit_id (BVA lower bound)
R-WEB-035: positive outcome on boundary input
tests.executable:
import pytest

MIN_BENEFIT_ID = 1

def test_manager_deletes_benefit_with_minimum_valid_id(
    api_client,          # <fixture>: authenticated requests.Session as manager
    ensure_benefit_id,   # <fixture>: ensures benefit with given id exists, cleans up on teardown
    benefits_url,        # <fixture>: base URL + '/benefits'
):
    ensure_benefit_id(MIN_BENEFIT_ID, name="Min ID Benefit")
    resp = api_client.delete(f"{benefits_url}/{MIN_BENEFIT_ID}")
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert str(body.get("id") or body.get("entity_id")) == str(MIN_BENEFIT_ID)
    verify = api_client.get(f"{benefits_url}/{MIN_BENEFIT_ID}")
    assert verify.status_code == 404, "Benefit id=1 must be gone after deletion"
    audit = <get_audit_record>(api_client, entity="benefit", entity_id=MIN_BENEFIT_ID, action="delete")
    assert audit is not None
    assert str(audit["entity_id"]) == str(MIN_BENEFIT_ID)

tests.name: test_delete_benefit_with_invalid_id_returns_400
actions: Submit DELETE request using benefit_id=0 (invalid, below minimum valid boundary)
expected: Response body contains a validation error message
trace: R-WEB-035: error pathway - 400 on validation error
R-WEB-035: negative test - invalid benefit_id value rejected by server
tests.executable:
import pytest

INVALID_BENEFIT_ID = 0

def test_delete_benefit_with_invalid_id_returns_400(
    api_client,          # <fixture>: authenticated requests.Session as manager
    benefits_url,        # <fixture>: base URL + '/benefits'
):
    resp = api_client.delete(f"{benefits_url}/{INVALID_BENEFIT_ID}")
    assert resp.status_code == 400, f"Expected 400 for invalid id=0, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    msg = body.get("message", "").lower()
    assert "invalid" in msg or "validation" in msg or "error" in msg, (
        f"Expected validation error message, got: {body}"
    )
    audit = <get_audit_record>(api_client, entity="benefit", entity_id=INVALID_BENEFIT_ID, action="delete")
    assert audit is None, "No audit record should exist for an invalid delete request"
